items.label: About ZYNQ.peer
items.content:
# ZYNQ.peer

Lightweight WebRTC wrapper built on PeerJS. Designed for fast peer-to-peer communication with messaging, audio, and video.

### Features

- Peer-to-peer messaging (1-to-1 + broadcast)
- Audio & video calls (WebRTC via PeerJS)
- Invite / accept / reject handshake system
- Session-based connection management
- Heartbeat + timeout system (auto disconnect stale peers)
- Local stream caching with reference counting
- Automatic stream reuse per configuration
- Bitrate monitoring (video calls)
- Event-driven API
- No backend required

items.label: Quick Start
items.content:
# Quick Start

### Setup
```javascript
import 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@0.3.0/index.js'
await ZYNQ.load('peer')
await ZYNQ.peer.start()
```

### Get your ID
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('ready', id => {
  console.log('My ID:', id)
})
```

### Connect
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.connect('peer-id', {
  video: true,
  audio: true
})
```

### Send message
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.sendTo('peer-id', 'Hello')
```

### Receive messages
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('message', ({ peerId, message }) => {
  console.log(peerId, message)
})
```

items.label: Start
items.content:
# Core API

### Start peer
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.start(customId?)
```

- Initializes PeerJS connection
- Emits `ready` when peer ID is assigned
- Starts heartbeat loop

---

### Connect
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.connect(peerId, config)
```

Config:
```javascript
{
  video: boolean,
  audio: boolean,
  resolution: 720,
  fps: 30
}
```

- Creates data connection + optional media call
- Triggers invite/accept flow

---

### Destroy
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.destroy()
```

- Closes all sessions
- Stops all streams
- Destroys PeerJS instance
- Clears memory and events

items.label: Events
items.content:
# Events

### Listen
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('event', callback)
```

### Event list

| Event | Data | Description |
|------|------|------------|
| `ready` | id | Peer is ready |
| `invite` | {peerId, config, accept, reject} | Incoming connection request |
| `joined` | peerId | Peer accepted connection |
| `left` | peerId | Peer disconnected |
| `message` | {peerId, message} | Chat message |
| `local-stream` | MediaStream | Local camera/mic stream created |
| `remote-stream` | {peerId, stream} | Incoming video/audio stream |
| `local-stream-ended` | {peerId?} | Stream released (refCount = 0) |
| `bitrate` | {peerId, kbps} | Video quality estimate |
| `rejected` | peerId | Invite rejected |
| `error` | {code, message, peerId?} | Runtime error |
| `destroyed` | void | Peer system destroyed |

### Notes
- `invite.accept()` must be called manually
- `invite.reject()` closes session
- Bitrate updates only apply to video calls

items.label: Messaging
items.content:
# Messaging

### Send to peer
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.sendTo(peerId, message)
```

### Broadcast
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.broadcast(message)
```

### Receive
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('message', ({ peerId, message }) => {})
```

Supports: string, object, array

items.label: Audio & Video
items.content:
# Media

### Connect with media
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.connect(peerId, {
  video: true,
  audio: true,
  resolution: 720,
  fps: 30
})
```

### Streams
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('local-stream', stream => {
  localVideo.srcObject = stream
})

ZYNQ.peer.on('remote-stream', ({ peerId, stream }) => {
  remoteVideo.srcObject = stream
})
```

### Stream system
- Streams are cached per configuration
- Reference counting prevents duplicates
- Streams are reused across sessions
- Automatically released when unused

items.label: Connections
items.content:
# Connections

### Disconnect peer
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.disconnect(peerId)
```

### Get my ID
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.getMyId()
```

### Active peers
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.getActivePeers()
```

Returns array of connected peer IDs

items.label: Exists
items.content:
# Exists

### Check if peer is online
```javascript
const online = await ZYNQ.peer.exists(peerId, 3000)
```

### Notes
- Best-effort reachability check using temporary connection
- Not a guaranteed presence system

items.label: System & Advanced
items.content:
# System

### Behavior
- Heartbeat: 2000ms
- Timeout: 12000ms inactivity
- Auto disconnect stale sessions
- WebRTC stats polling for bitrate

### Stream system
- Config-based stream keys
- RefCount-based lifecycle
- Safe cleanup on disconnect

### Debug
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setDebug(0 | 1 | 2)
```

### Auto start
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setAutoStart(false)
```
